content: Hi, if we use Box::<T>, where do we put it in mem? is it equivalent to kmalloc with GFP_KERNEL??

Fox Chen said:

Hi, if we use Box::<T>, where do we put it in mem? is it equivalent to kmalloc with GFP_KERNEL??

If I am not wrong, allocator uses GFP_KERNEL by default.

Douglas Su said:

Fox Chen said:

Hi, if we use Box::<T>, where do we put it in mem? is it equivalent to kmalloc with GFP_KERNEL??

If I am not wrong, allocator uses GFP_KERNEL by default.

thank you.

Just for my curiousity. How do I use different flags??

Currently, you cannot (unless you manually use a different allocator etc.). How to handle this is part of the alloc topic which is going on (alongside making all allocations fallible etc.). We discussed it on Monday where we reached consensus on the approach to take, so we will have a solution soon!
This topic was moved by Miguel Ojeda to #Library > alloc
